edge_anomaly: THE "1 EDGE" IS A MEASUREMENT ARTIFACT, NOT A GENERATION FAILURE. work/qP-9wwRrJbg/structure.json stores the graph as an OBJECT: "dependencies": {"edges": [...]} — len(structure["dependencies"]) == 1 because the dict has one key. The actual edge list contains 286 edges for 139 units (2.06/unit), fully comparable to dHjWVlfNraM's 136 edges for 61 units (2.23/unit), which also uses the identical {"edges": [...]} shape. Every derivation pass fired: rule=190 (127 continues, 43 requires, 13 visually_depends_on, 4 answers, 3 refers_to), bridge=18, llm=78 (58 explains, 13 answers, 3 illustrates, 3 summarizes, 1 continues) — so the LLM refinement pass did NOT silently fail (backend/pipeline/understand/dependencies.py:191-195 wraps it in try/except; a failure would leave 0 llm-derivation edges, and there are 78). The only other way to read "1" out of this file is the single ('llm','continues') edge. The artifact is the one the 2-clip run consumed: structure.json mtime 18:07:52 sits between punctuation (18:03) and the assembly, in the same job. CAVEAT/REAL DEFICIENCY the anomaly hunt should have found instead: (i) prerequisite ("requires") density is HALF of dHjWVlfNraM — 43 rule + 18 bridge = 61 requires-edges over 139 units (0.44/unit) vs 39+23=62 over 61 units (1.02/unit) — because concepts_introduced is sparse (only 20/139 units introduce anything, 25 concepts total, vs 12/61 units / 16 concepts) and both _rule_edges (dependencies.py:47-51) and _bridge_edges (dependencies.py:119-124) can only target units that DECLARE concepts_introduced; (ii) the LLM edge pass is FORBIDDEN from adding 'requires' by its own prompt — dependencies.py:163-165 asks only for "explains, illustrates, contradicts, summarizes, answers" — so the missed chains the inventory flags (UAM equations -> free fall -> projectile y-direction; components example -> "resolve v_i") have no mechanism that could ever emit them. This degrades closure targets, context cards, and prerequisite hints (clip 2's single "prerequisite [1]" hint is a symptom), but it is NOT why only 2 clips shipped.
miss_classification: METHOD: no run ledger is persisted, so I re-executed the deterministic Wave-1 path offline (pre-Wave-1 backup .backup/backend-pre-wave1-2026-07-02.tgz, whose select_anchors is byte-identical to the current legacy path candidates.py:93-107, documented "byte-equivalent"; LLM calls hard-blocked) against the cached structure + punctuation. KEY NUMBERS: 75 of 139 units are anchor-eligible; priorities (backend/adapters/base.py:64-74, adapters/lecture.py:21-47): result=80, claim=70, definition=68, eq_intro=64, solution=60, procedure=55, practice_prompt=52, physical_interpretation=46, explanation=45. Score = priority*(0.4+0.6*rel)*(0.5+0.5*conf) (candidates.py:105), flat-sorted (:106), top-12 (:107, config.py:210 MAX_ANCHORS=12). Under uniform relevance, 52 units (27 claim + 12 definition + 6 eq_intro + 4 solution + 2 procedure + 1 result) outrank the BEST practice_prompt — all 12 practice_prompts are mathematically unreachable at cap 12; 39 claim+definition units alone triple the cap. Python's stable sort breaks the massive 0.70-priority tie by list order = TEMPORAL order, so the reconstructed 12 anchors are ALL in the first 281s of a 1432s video (1 result + the 11 earliest claims). Then snap/dedupe (boundary_adapt.py:83-119) collapsed 12 anchors -> 3 specs: 8 candidates dropped as "overlap loser" (:94), 1 merged — adjacent anchors' closures (127 'continues' edges chain same-node neighbors) are near-identical spans. The real run's LLM relevance perturbs this: shipped spans (106.87-324.28, 441.72-551.06) show the actual top-12 sat in the vectors/defs region (23 eligible units in 100-345s) and the UAM region (u0054-u0059), nothing before 100s or after 551s. PER-ITEM: [3][4][5][6] + first half of [7] = clip 1; [10][11][12][13] = clip 2 (both are multi-item mashes — class (d) merging, not one-item clips). [1] sig-figs (u0003 claim, prio 70, conf 1.0 — anchors under uniform rel) and [2] conversion (u0011 result, prio 80 = the HIGHEST-scored unit in the whole video under uniform rel): did NOT ship despite top priority — class (c) with high confidence: topic="kinematics" relevance scored sig-figs/unit-conversion low, either < ANCHOR_REL_FLOOR=0.25 (config.py:212, candidates.py:102-104) or rank-out via the (0.4+0.6*rel) term; the only alternative is (b) a post-merge judge kill of a 7-93s spec (confidence low — nothing suggests it anchored). [7] tail (u0049, instantaneous acceleration, 324.3-344.9): class (a) rank-out — cap exhausted before u0049; the merged clip's right edge is the last anchored unit's closure (confidence medium). [8][9] (u0052 eq_intro=64, u0053 definition=68, 346-441s): class (a) rank-out under the cap — outranked by 0.70 claims and earlier same-priority defs via stable-sort tie-break (confidence high: clip 2 starts at exactly 441.72=u0054). [14][15] graph slopes/areas (u0072/u0077 def=68, u0078 claim=70 exist!): class (a) rank-out, same tie-break front-loading (high). [16] graph-build worked example (u0080-u0103): class (a) STRUCTURAL — its 10 steps are worked_step (non-anchor role, base.py:71), its payoffs were never labeled result/solution; the span's only eligible roles are practice_prompts (unreachable) and 2 mid-arc claims that rank out (high confidence). [17] (u0103/u0104 physical_interpretation=46): class (a) mathematically unreachable — 52+ units outrank priority 46 (high). [18] components example (u0107-u0113 = worked_steps): class (a) non-anchor roles (high). [19] misconception u0113 (misconception is NOT an anchor role, base.py) + u0114 correction=50 ranked out: class (a) (high). [20][21][22] (u0118 def=68, u0120/u0131 procedure=55, u0121-u0130 claims=70): class (a) rank-out — eligible on paper, lost the 12-slot race to earlier ties (high; zero anchors past 551s empirically). [23][24][25] (u0133 def, u0134 claim eligible): class (a) rank-out; had they anchored, the unit-boundary drift (u0132-u0138 shifted ~1 sentence; u0138 labels the outro as equation_introduction) would likely have produced a (b) judge kill (high for (a)). [26] (inside u0137 worked_step): class (a) — no exam_tip-like role exists in the taxonomy; the content is invisible to role-based anchoring (high). CLASS (b) judge/repair kills: approximately ZERO in this run — the two shipped spans account for all plausible top-12 closures after merging; the asymmetric quote-verified gate (validate.py:12-13, 201-226) even PASSED two 3-5-topic mash clips, because it tests self-containedness, never atomicity or over-length (medium-high confidence). CLASS (d): the second-biggest killer — in reconstruction 9 of 12 anchor slots were consumed by candidates that dedupe absorbed into a neighbor, so 12 anchor slots yielded 3 spans; dropped losers do NOT free cap slots for un-anchored regions. SUMMARY: ~14 items class (a) (of which 4 are role-taxonomy/structural: 16, 18, 19, 26), 2-3 items class (c) ([1],[2],[26]-partially), ~0 items class (b), and the shipped-but-mashed/truncated shape of items 3-7/10-13 is class (d).
wave2_coverage: Assessed against the actual Wave-2 code in the tree (candidates.py:110-305 enforce_plan/select_anchors_planned, understand/arcs.py, assemble/plan.py) plus a live run of the deterministic arc detector on this structure. (1) EXTRACTION PLAN (plan.py, ANCHOR_SELECTOR="plan" default config.py:221): addresses class (a) rank-out for items 8, 9, 14, 15, 20, 21, 22, 23-25 — the model sees the full unit inventory + content map and proposes most-valuable-first, so back-half defs/claims/procedures can be proposed regardless of temporal tie-breaks. PARTIAL: still validated-then-capped at MAX_ANCHORS=12 (plan.py docstring, enforce_plan (iv) candidates.py:257-267), so at most ~12 of the ideal 20 can survive; also plan quality is an unverified LLM behavior. (2) TOPIC-COVERAGE QUOTAS (candidates.py:243-255): this video has 12 topic nodes (c0.t0-c1.t11 incl. Analyzing Motion Graphs, Two-Dimensional Motion, Projectile Motion, Relative Motion) and every one contains anchor-eligible units, so the coverage floor GUARANTEES >=1 anchor in each dead zone — fully fixes the "entire back 15 minutes shipped nothing" failure at topic granularity, but 12 topics x quota(ceil(12/12)+1=2) wants ~24 slots vs cap 12, so item-level coverage stays partial. (3) ROLE SATURATION (PLAN_ROLE_CAP=4, config.py:222; candidates.py:230): fully fixes the 27-claims-crowd-out; claims can hold at most 4 of 12 slots. (4) ARC DETECTION (arcs.py; detect_arcs run live on this structure): finds exactly 5 arcs — arc_0 = the unit-conversion worked example u0007-u0011 (recovers item [2], and since arcs are auto-appended and never dropped by saturation, candidates.py:200-205/227, it also bypasses the class-(c) relevance floor — planned/arc entries are never checked against _anchor_eligible, only coverage additions are, candidates.py:249) — item [2] FULLY addressed; arc_1-4 = the four 1-9s Socratic practice_pairs (u0062/63, u0070/71, u0073/74, u0075/76) which the inventory explicitly calls NON-clips. It does NOT detect the three big missing worked examples — [16] graph build (steps=worked_step, terminal labeled claim/physical_interpretation), [18] components (all worked_step, no result/solution), [24][25] relative motion (example_setup->problem_givens->worked_step, no terminal) — because TERMINAL_ROLES={result,solution} (+calculation-as-final) never matches how this labeler ended them. So arcs address the worked-example miss class for 1 of 5 real examples. (5) CONTRACT-BY-CONTENT + TRIM-CAPABLE REPAIR: address class (b), which killed ~nothing here — neutral for this video's coverage; trim may fix clip 1's mid-sentence start and the [7] truncation shape. (6) DEDUPE PREFERRING COMPLETE ARCS: fixes the winner-choice inside class (d) (an arc beats a fragment) but does NOT prevent adjacent planned anchors' closures from merging into multi-item mashes, and dropped overlap losers still don't refund cap slots. NET: Wave 2 plausibly turns 2 clips into ~8-12 with all 12 topics represented — a real fix for the complaint's headline — but cannot reach the ideal 18-22, cannot see 3 of 5 worked examples, and spends 4 protected arc slots on micro practice-pairs.
unaddressed: (1) MAX_ANCHORS=12 AS A HARD CEILING (config.py:210): every Wave-2 mechanism funnels into the same cap (plan validation, enforce_plan step (iv) candidates.py:257-267 — even arcs are cap-evicted at :262-263). This video's defensible inventory is 18-22 clips; 12 is a structural impossibility, and the 4 detected micro practice-pair arcs (protected from saturation, candidates.py:227) can consume a third of it. No in-flight mechanism scales the cap with unit count (139), anchor-eligible count (75), topic-node count (12), or detection density="high". (2) NO CONTENT-DRIVEN CLIP-COUNT POLICY anywhere: max_clips=MAX_SEGMENTS=12 (config.py:112) and MAX_ANCHORS are constants; the ideal count is a property of the video (this one: ~20), and nothing in Wave 1 or 2 estimates it. (3) ARC-GRAMMAR BRITTLENESS TO LABELING: 3 of 5 real worked examples here terminate in worked_step/claim/physical_interpretation and are invisible to arcs.py's TERMINAL_ROLES={result,solution}; conversely 4 of 5 detected "arcs" are 2-9-second Socratic non-clips. The arc mechanism inherits the role-labeler's noise with no span-level (timing/visual) corroboration and no minimum-substance filter. (4) ROLE TAXONOMY GAPS + UNIT DRIFT: no exam_tip-like role (items [1][26] labeled claim/buried in worked_step), misconception is a non-anchor role (item [19]), and the tail units u0132-u0138 are shifted ~1 sentence (u0138 labels the outro as equation_introduction) — Wave 2's contract-by-content binds contracts to better evidence but nothing re-audits roles or unit boundaries; a plan built on wrong labels proposes wrong things. (5) PREREQUISITE GRAPH: the LLM edge pass structurally cannot emit 'requires' (dependencies.py:163-165) and rule/bridge edges are starved by sparse concepts_introduced (20/139 units); Wave 2 does not touch dependencies.py — cards/prereq hints/closure targeting stay weak (0.44 vs 1.02 requires-edges/unit on the comparison video). (6) MASH/ATOMICITY: the judge has no over-inclusion or one-idea-per-clip criterion (validate.py gate = topic/purpose/grounded/references), and dedupe still merges same-facet overlaps (boundary_adapt.py:119) — clip 1 was a ~5-ideal-clip, 217s mash that PASSED; Wave 2's arc-preferring dedupe doesn't split it. (7) DEDUPE DOESN'T REFUND THE CAP: 9 of 12 slots evaporated as overlap losers in reconstruction; no re-fill loop from the ranked remainder exists in either wave. (8) JUDGE THROUGHPUT: raising the cap to ~20-30 candidates multiplies judge cost — each candidate is 1 judge + up to JUDGE_MAX_REPAIR=3 re-judges + kill-confirmation calls (validate.py, config.py JUDGE_WORKERS=4) on gemini-2.5-flash-lite free-tier limits; no batched judging exists. (9) TOPIC-FILTER SEMANTICS: "kinematics" on an all-kinematics video is a no-op that costs an LLM pass and still kills tangential-but-valuable items ([1] sig-figs exam tip); no policy distinguishes "search within video" from "give me everything important" — the user's actual ask.
quick_wins: (1) SCALE MAX_ANCHORS WITH CONTENT (config.py:210): e.g. cap = clamp(12, n_anchor_eligible_units/4 or n_units/8, 32) or += for detection.density=="high" (this video: detection says density=high, 75 eligible units, 12 topic nodes -> cap ~19-24, matching the ideal 18-22). Evidence: enforce_plan's own quota arithmetic (candidates.py:220, ceil(cap/n_topics)+1) already wants ~2/topic x 12 topics = 24 slots; the constant 12 is the binding constraint everywhere. One-line config change; judge cost scales linearly (mitigate via JUDGE_WORKERS, config.py). (2) RELEVANCE BYPASS WHEN QUERY == DETECTED TOPIC: score_topic_relevance already short-circuits to all-1.0 for empty topic (candidates.py:62-63); add the same short-circuit when the query fuzzy-matches detection rationale/title/content-map root ("kinematics" appears verbatim in detection.rationale and the video title). Kills the class-(c) losses ([1],[2]) and removes LLM relevance noise from tie-breaking. (3) BREAK SCORE TIES BY TOPIC-NODE SPREAD, NOT TIME: the flat sort (candidates.py:106) resolves the 27-way 0.70 tie by list order = front of video; a secondary key that round-robins node_id (or penalizes an already-selected unit's node) is ~3 lines in the legacy selector and materially de-front-loads even without the plan path. (4) PER-ROLE CAP IN THE LEGACY SELECTOR: PLAN_ROLE_CAP=4 (config.py:222) only guards the plan path; applying the same cap inside select_anchors (candidates.py:97-105) protects the "priority" A/B arm and any plan-fallback run (this run WAS effectively that arm). (5) REFUND DEDUPE LOSSES: after snap_candidates, if len(specs) < cap, re-run selection excluding units already covered by shipped spans and assemble another round (assemble/__init__.py after :109 in the pre-Wave-1 layout) — in reconstruction this alone would have turned 3 specs into ~10-12, since 63 eligible units remained untouched. (6) RAISE BRIDGE/CONCEPT RECALL CHEAPLY: BRIDGE_MAX_PER_UNIT=6 (config.py:194) is not the limiter — sparse concepts_introduced is; a one-line prompt nudge in the unit-extraction pass to always name introduced concepts for definition/equation_introduction units would roughly double requires-edge density toward the comparison video's 1.02/unit. (7) ANCHOR_MIN_PRIORITY=40 (config.py:211) is currently decorative — under cap 12 nothing below ~64 ever wins; if the cap is raised, revisit priorities so physical_interpretation(46)/explanation(45) items ([17], [9]-adjacent) can actually land.
